title: Quiz: QAOA Parameter Optimization
description: Test your understanding of QAOA parameter optimization strategies

questions.question: Why are gradient-free optimizers often preferred for QAOA on noisy quantum devices?

options.text: They don't require gradient estimation which is costly and noisy

questions.explanation: Gradient-free methods like COBYLA don't require estimating gradients, which on quantum hardware requires additional circuit executions and is susceptible to noise.

questions.question: In linear ramp initialization, how do γ and β parameters typically change with layer index?
questions.explanation: Linear ramp mimics adiabatic evolution: β (mixer) starts strong and decreases, while γ (cost) starts weak and increases, gradually transitioning from mixer-dominated to cost-dominated.

questions.question: What is the purpose of multistart optimization in QAOA?

options.text: To avoid getting trapped in local minima
To decrease the number of qubits

questions.explanation: Multistart optimization runs the optimizer from multiple random initial points to explore different regions of the parameter landscape and avoid local minima.

questions.question: Parameter transfer from p=2 to p=4 QAOA uses interpolation to create the new parameters.
questions.explanation: Depth transfer uses interpolation to adapt parameters from one depth to another, maintaining the general structure of the parameter schedule.

questions.question: What does SPSA stand for and why is it useful for QAOA?

options.text: Sequential Parameter Search Algorithm - it's faster
Simultaneous Perturbation Stochastic Approximation - robust to noise
Simplified Parameter Selection Approach - reduces complexity
Standard Parametric Spectral Analysis - improves accuracy

questions.explanation: SPSA (Simultaneous Perturbation Stochastic Approximation) estimates gradients using only 2 function evaluations regardless of dimension, and is robust to noisy measurements.

questions.question: What is FOURIER initialization in QAOA?

options.text: Using Fourier transform of the cost function
Parameterizing angles using low-frequency Fourier components

questions.explanation: FOURIER initialization uses low-frequency Fourier components to parameterize smooth angle schedules, reducing the number of free parameters while maintaining expressiveness.

questions.question: When does parameter transfer between problem instances work best?

options.text: When problems have very different structures
When problems have similar structure and size

questions.explanation: Parameter transfer works best when source and target problems have similar structure (e.g., both are MaxCut on similar graphs), as optimal parameters tend to be similar.

questions.question: Which strategies help deal with shot noise in QAOA optimization? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Averaging (a) reduces variance, gradient-free methods (b) avoid noisy gradient estimation, and SPSA (d) is specifically designed for noisy settings. Circuit depth (c) doesn't help with shot noise.
